marketState: neutral-to-cautiously-bullish - weather-driven demand strength and crowded short positioning offset by storage surplus and bearish daily/weekly trend
summary: Front-month NG ($2.911) is trading essentially at fair value across every horizon (FAIR status now through +14D, only SLIGHTLY UNDER on the next contract at +21D), so there is no large mispricing to trade against. The standout signal is weather: the 8-16 day demand outlook is +31.1% vs the 5-year norm and sits above the historical max range (rangePos 1.11), a real bullish catalyst that the fair-value model has only partially absorbed (fair rises from $2.923 today to $3.055 by +21D). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years - a crowded short that raises squeeze risk if the hot demand forecast verifies. Working against this: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, seasonality reliability is low with a mild bearish tilt (only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%), and daily/weekly technical trend is bearish. Net-net, the medium-term setup skews cautiously bullish on weather and positioning, while the 1-10 day picture lacks a clean directional edge since price sits right on fair value with conflicting technicals across timeframes.

longTerm.entry: $2.85 - $2.95 (near current front price, scale in on dips within zone)
longTerm.rationale: 8-16 day demand forecast is above its 5-year max range and managed money is net short at the 8th percentile of 5 years - a combination that favors upside if hot demand verifies and shorts cover, while the rising fair-value path ($2.923 to $3.055 over 21 days) supports a grind higher. Storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y) and low-reliability, mildly bearish seasonality (38% odds higher in 3-4 weeks) argue for modest conviction and a defined stop rather than an aggressive size.
longTerm.invalidation: Demand_8_16D forecast fades back inside the normal range, storage surprises keep printing bearish/larger builds, or price closes below $2.75 confirming the bearish weekly trend is intact.

shortTerm.rationale: Price sits right at fair value (FAIR status, spread only -$0.012) so there is no mean-reversion edge, and technicals are internally conflicted - bullish on 15m but bearish on 4h/1d/1w with RSI(14) neutral (37-58 across timeframes). The futures curve shows only slight backwardation front-to-Sep (-$0.035), no strong near-term signal either way.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.75-$2.85 fair-value support zone with bullish confirmation (RSI turning up, MACD histogram flipping positive on 4h/1d) to go long, or a daily close below $2.85 with bearish EMA/MACD alignment across 4h-1w to go short.
